title: InvocationEnvelope
description: The V0 stage invocation envelope: what the runner hands a stage when the workflow advances (ARCHITECTURE.md §5). Carries context POINTERS only — never upstream result bodies (§2.4).

triggerRef.description: The event or item that caused the run. Bounded scheduler metadata only, never the provider's raw trigger payload.

branchNamespace.description: The gaggle's run-branch namespace root (GaggleSpec.BranchNamespace); empty means the default 'goobers/'. Injected as GOOBERS_BRANCH_NAMESPACE (#965/#1010).

instructionAddendum.description: An operator-supplied, one-off addition to the agent instructions for this invocation. Never persisted to the workflow definition.

workspace.description: Absolute path to the fresh, isolated, disposable working copy the stage runs in (§5).

contextPointers.description: Read-only inputs: journal artifact pointers and external refs. Pointers only.

capabilities.description: Declared capability grants (e.g. 'github:issues:write'). Undeclared use fails closed (§5).

inputs.description: The stage's static config from its definition. The stage's own config — not another stage's state. Unlike result.schema.json's outputs (scalar-bounded by schema), inputs is intentionally unconstrained: the anti-smuggling invariant here is convention-backed, not schema-enforced, because the runner deliberately routes a gate's subject-stage status/outputs through a gate's own inputs (internal/runner's flattening into env.Inputs before an automated/agentic gate evaluation, internal/gate.InputKeyStatus) — a small, scalar-only, one-directional bridge, not the general reach-through §2.4 forbids (#125).

contextPointer.description: Exactly one of artifact or external.

runId.description: The run whose journal `artifact` lives in, when it is NOT this stage's own run (cross-run evidence resolution, #103/T3). Empty/absent means the artifact is this run's own upstream output. A non-empty value crosses a trust boundary and requires the journal:read capability, enforced by the harness. Mirrors ContextPointer.RunID (Go); present here so a valid cross-run envelope passes this closed schema (#125).
